encounter.reason: 2-week follow-up after RIPE therapy initiation; DOT enrollment; baseline visual acuity for ethambutol monitoring
encounter.location: UI Health Ambulatory TB Clinic, 1801 W Taylor St, Suite 1E, Chicago, IL 60612

labs.name: aspartate aminotransferase (AST)
labs.notes: Mildly elevated from baseline of 28 at admission; within acceptable limits for RIPE therapy, less than 3× ULN

labs.name: alanine aminotransferase (ALT)
labs.notes: Mildly elevated from baseline of 32 at admission; within acceptable limits for RIPE therapy, less than 3× ULN

labs.name: hemoglobin
labs.notes: Mild normocytic anemia; slightly improved from 11.2 at admission

labs.name: glucose, fasting
labs.notes: Elevated; reflects diabetes management concern with rifampin-metformin interaction

labs.name: albumin
labs.notes: Mildly low; likely related to chronic infection/inflammation

labs.name: uric acid
labs.notes: Upper normal to mildly elevated; baseline for pyrazinamide monitoring

labs.name: sputum AFB smear
labs.notes: Positive but decreased from 2+ at admission — fewer organisms on smear

screening.name: PHQ-2 depression screening
screening.interpretation: Low risk; no further screening indicated. Patient endorsed "little interest or pleasure in doing things" as "several days," attributed to isolation at home during infectious period.

conditions.name: pulmonary tuberculosis, active
conditions.notes: Clinically improving — cough less productive, afebrile, AFB smear burden decreasing from 2+ to 1+. LFTs mildly elevated but acceptable. Continuing RIPE therapy.

conditions.name: type 2 diabetes mellitus
conditions.notes: Fasting glucose 172 mg/dL (elevated). Rifampin may reduce metformin efficacy via CYP enzyme induction. Close glucose monitoring recommended.

conditions.name: essential hypertension
conditions.notes: BP 136/82 mmHg, controlled on current lisinopril regimen.

conditions.name: chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), mild
conditions.notes: Stable, no exacerbation, not requiring increased rescue inhaler use.

conditions.name: mild normocytic anemia
conditions.notes: First identified at admission (Hgb 11.2). At this encounter Hgb 11.5 — slightly improved. Likely anemia of chronic disease/infection.

medications.name: pyridoxine (vitamin B6) 50 mg oral, once daily
ethambutol 1200 mg oral, once daily
medications.notes: Baseline visual acuity documented at this visit for monitoring.

medications.name: metformin 1000 mg oral, twice daily
medications.notes: PharmD David Chen flagged rifampin-metformin interaction (rifampin may reduce metformin efficacy via CYP enzyme induction); close glucose monitoring recommended.

medications.name: albuterol HFA metered-dose inhaler, as needed
medications.sig: Inhale 1–2 puffs by mouth as needed for shortness of breath

document_references.description: Outpatient infectious disease follow-up note by Dr. Lena Okafor documenting 2-week post-discharge assessment of pulmonary TB on RIPE therapy, clinical improvement, lab review, AFB smear results, DOT enrollment, and plan for continued monitoring with 2-week return visit.

Documentation of Robert's enrollment in the directly observed therapy (DOT) program at UI Health Ambulatory TB Clinic. Monday–Friday observed administration at clinic; weekend self-administration with pill counts and medication diary reviewed on Mondays.

Baseline visual acuity documentation for ethambutol ocular toxicity monitoring: 20/25 OD, 20/25 OS by Snellen chart; color vision intact on Ishihara plate testing. Established as reference for future comparison.

Education materials provided covering TB treatment expectations, medication side effects (GI upset, orange secretions from rifampin, hepatotoxicity warning signs, visual changes with ethambutol), and respiratory isolation guidance during infectious period.

diagnostic_reports.description: Only AST and ALT values reported from this panel (2 of ~7 typical components). LFTs mildly elevated from admission baseline (AST 35 vs 28, ALT 38 vs 32) but within acceptable limits for continued RIPE therapy (less than 3× ULN).
Only selected values reported: creatinine 1.0 mg/dL (normal), fasting glucose 172 mg/dL (elevated), albumin 3.1 g/dL (mildly low). Only 3 of ~14 typical CMP components provided.
Sputum specimen collected at this visit and sent to mycobacteriology lab. Culture results pending (expected ~3 weeks). Inpatient sputum cultures from admission also still pending.
